record.text: The plot: Jimmy was a down on his luck egg farmer, raised by butterflies. Margaret was a rich heiress whose family was lost in the Eggvalanche of ‘26. She wanted to eat Jimmy’s family in revenge until he showed her how to use an abacus. He proposed the day she learned how to add. 💖

images.alt: a mock up of a vintage style movie poster, with a bright yellow background featuring red, white, and black elements. the title reads "Please Margaret NO" and shows a well groomed man in a suit looking terrified, as a woman with curled hair and red lipstick holds her mouth open in a broad smile. the text below reads "he was a man with the world's largest collection of live butterflies and she wanted to eat them all". a motif of black and white butterfly silhouettes floats along the top of the poster.

images.alt: "Cracked Ice" is inspired by February in Ukraine. The word "лютий" in the Ukrainian language is an adjective that means "very angry" or "furious." This month is particularly harsh because it is shorter than the others. It seems to want winter to last all year, even though spring is coming! The colours used are cool shades of light and dark grey-blue. The edges of the ice chunks are intentionally sharp and aggressive-looking, while the deep blue represents the danger of the ice-cold depths below.

"Cracked Ice in room setting " is inspired by February in Ukraine. The word "лютий" in the Ukrainian language is an adjective that means "very angry" or "furious." This month is particularly harsh because it is shorter than the others. It seems to want winter to last all year, even though spring is coming! The colors used are cool shades of light and dark grey-blue. The edges of the ice chunks are intentionally sharp and aggressive-looking, while the deep blue represents the danger of the ice-cold depths below.

record.text:

images.alt: "Cracked Ice" is inspired by February in Ukraine. The word "лютий" in the Ukrainian language is an adjective that means "very angry" or "furious." This month is particularly harsh because it is shorter than the others. It seems to want winter to last all year, even though spring is coming! The colours used are cool shades of light and dark grey-blue. The edges of the ice chunks are intentionally sharp and aggressive-looking, while the deep blue represents the danger of the ice-cold depths below.

"Cracked Ice in room setting " is inspired by February in Ukraine. The word "лютий" in the Ukrainian language is an adjective that means "very angry" or "furious." This month is particularly harsh because it is shorter than the others. It seems to want winter to last all year, even though spring is coming! The colors used are cool shades of light and dark grey-blue. The edges of the ice chunks are intentionally sharp and aggressive-looking, while the deep blue represents the danger of the ice-cold depths below.

record.text: It's so frustrating, isn't it? The people who provocatively use pejorative terms like 'red tape' do so knowing that it shifts the focus on how regulations stop you from doing things rather than, for the most part, how they protect you (and your family) from other people doing bad things to you.

images.alt: A screencapture of Wikipedia showing a chart of net imports by country. The top line is World total. The world trade balance, which equals total imports minus total exports, is not zero under three different measurement systems, in fact it is either a deficit or a surplus in the hundreds of billions of dollars. Since every imported good was exported from another place, the global trade balance should equal 0.
